---
title: "3D_house_model_rhino8"
author: "Alejandra Vouga"
date: "2026-04-09"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r}
# 1. Install necessary packages (if not already installed)
if (!require("plotly")) install.packages("plotly")
if (!require("readobj")) install.packages("readobj")

# 2. Load the libraries
library(plotly)
library(readobj)

# 3. Set your folder as the working directory
setwd("~/Desktop/github")

# 4. Define the path and load the model
obj_path <- "house_model.obj"
house_data <- read.obj(obj_path)

# 5. Extract components for the 3D plot
# We loop through the 'shapes' in your OBJ to build the house
fig <- plot_ly()

for (s in 1:length(house_data$shapes)) {
  shape <- house_data$shapes[[s]]
  
  fig <- fig %>% add_trace(
    type = 'mesh3d',
    x = shape$positions[1,],
    y = shape$positions[2,],
    z = shape$positions[3,],
    i = shape$indices[1,],
    j = shape$indices[2,],
    k = shape$indices[3,],
    opacity = 1,
    name = paste("Part", s)
  )
}

# 6. Final Layout and Viewing Adjustment
fig <- fig %>% layout(
  scene = list(
    aspectmode = "data", # Keeps the house shape from stretching
    xaxis = list(visible = FALSE),
    yaxis = list(visible = FALSE),
    zaxis = list(visible = FALSE)
  )
)

# 7. Show the interactive model in the RStudio Viewer tab
fig


```
